name: Todo List MCP Server
what_it_does: Todo list MCP server by glaucia86 (Glaucia Lemos — a Microsoft Developer Advocate). Provides AI agents with CRUD operations on a local todo list — creating, reading, updating, and completing todo items with a clean, well-documented implementation suitable for learning and personal productivity use.
use_cases: Managing personal todo lists from AI productivity agents
Demonstrating MCP server implementation patterns for learning
Lightweight task tracking for individual developers and agents
Building simple AI-driven productivity workflows with todo management
not_for: Enterprise task management (use Jira, Linear, or Todoist MCPs)
best_when: An individual developer wants a simple, well-implemented todo MCP for personal productivity or as a clean reference implementation for learning how to build MCP servers.
avoid_when: You need enterprise task management or multi-user collaboration. Data is stored locally — not synced across machines.

auth.notes: Local todo storage — no authentication required.

pricing.notes: Free open source by Microsoft Developer Advocate glaucia86.

known_agent_gotchas: Local storage — todos not persisted across MCP server restarts unless storage is configured
glaucia86 is a Microsoft Developer Advocate — implementation quality is higher than typical community tools
Simple schema — verify it supports all task fields you need before adopting
Intended partly as a learning example — may not have all production features
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local todo storage. No credentials. Todo data stays local. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local storage. No credentials. Low risk.
